title: install.sh — v2 contract compliance
body:
All 6 install.sh changes from the planning doc:

1. Env vars: Use VENDOR_REF/VENDOR_REPO with positional fallback
2. Install dir: Use VENDOR_INSTALL_DIR with .semver/ fallback for code files
3. File placement: fetch code files to $INSTALL_DIR, config stays in .semver/
4. Remove .semver/.version write (framework owns version tracking)
5. Remove .vendored/config.json self-registration block (framework handles registration)
6. Manifest emission: track INSTALLED_FILES array, write to $VENDOR_MANIFEST when set

See docs/planning/vendor-v2-compliance.md sections 1-6 for before/after code.

title: git-semver — vendored config loading
body:
Update load_config() in git-semver to support the v2 config path:

1. Try .vendored/configs/git-semver.json first (framework-managed copy)
2. Fall back to .semver/config.json (direct/legacy installs)
3. Filter out _vendor key from loaded config (framework-owned metadata)
4. --config CLI flag still works as explicit override (skips vendored lookup)

Add VENDORED_CONFIG constant alongside DEFAULT_CONFIG_PATH.

See docs/planning/vendor-v2-compliance.md 'Config loading' section for the target code.

title: Update tests for v2 contract
body:
Update existing tests to match v2 changes:

test_install.py:
- Remove assertion for .semver/.version existence (test_creates_semver_directory_and_files line 81)
- Remove test_version_file_contains_version (or repurpose)
- Remove/update test_always_updates_core_script_and_version .version assertions
- Add tests for VENDOR_REF/VENDOR_REPO env var usage (with and without)
- Add tests for VENDOR_INSTALL_DIR (code files go to custom dir, config stays in .semver/)
- Add tests for VENDOR_MANIFEST emission (file written when env var set, not written when unset)
- Add test that self-registration block is gone (no .vendored/config.json modification)

test_config.py:
- Add tests for vendored config path (.vendored/configs/git-semver.json tried first)
- Add test for _vendor key filtering
- Add test that --config flag overrides vendored path
- Add test for fallback when vendored config doesn't exist

title: Update README and docs for v2 changes
body:
Update documentation to reflect v2 contract changes:

README.md:
- Remove .semver/.version from 'What gets installed' tree (line 43)
- Update File Classification table: remove .semver/.version row (line 322)
- Note that code files may live in .vendored/pkg/git-semver/ when installed via framework

docs/planning/vendor-v2-compliance.md:
- Mark checklist items as complete
- Add 'Implemented in <epic-id>' header

title: install.sh — v2 contract compliance
body:
Make install.sh compliant with the git-vendored v2 contract. All changes are in install.sh and test_install.py.

## Changes

### Env vars (replace hardcoded values)
- Use `VENDOR_REF` for version (`$1` as fallback for old frameworks / direct usage)
- Use `VENDOR_REPO` for repo (hardcode `mangimangi/git-semver` as fallback)

### Install directory
- Use `VENDOR_INSTALL_DIR` for code file destination (`.semver/` as fallback)
- Code files (`git-semver`, `bump-and-release`) go to `$INSTALL_DIR/`
- Config stays in `.semver/config.json` (never moves)
- Workflow stays in `.github/workflows/` (never moves)

### Removals
- Delete `.semver/.version` write (`echo "$VERSION" > .semver/.version`)
- Delete entire `.vendored/config.json` self-registration block (lines 71-89)

### Manifest emission
- Track installed code files in `INSTALLED_FILES` array
- Write manifest to `$VENDOR_MANIFEST` when the env var is set
- Config and workflow are NOT added to manifest (user-editable files)

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `VENDOR_REF` env var used for version, positional `$1` as fallback
- [ ] `VENDOR_REPO` env var used for repo, hardcoded fallback
- [ ] `VENDOR_INSTALL_DIR` used for code file destination, `.semver/` fallback
- [ ] `git-semver` and `bump-and-release` fetched to `$INSTALL_DIR/`
- [ ] Config created in `.semver/config.json` (unchanged)
- [ ] `.semver/.version` write removed
- [ ] Self-registration block removed
- [ ] `INSTALLED_FILES` array tracks installed code files
- [ ] Manifest written to `$VENDOR_MANIFEST` when set
- [ ] No manifest written when `$VENDOR_MANIFEST` is unset
- [ ] Backwards compatible: works with no env vars set (old framework / direct use)
- [ ] Tests updated: remove `.version` assertions, add env var tests, add manifest tests, verify no self-registration

## References

- See `docs/planning/epics/gsv-9ed5.md` sections 1-6 for before/after code
- Current install.sh: 93 lines, straightforward bash

title: git-semver — vendored config loading
body:
Update load_config() in git-semver to support the v2 vendored config path. All changes are in git-semver (Python) and test_config.py.

## Changes

### Config loading
- Add `VENDORED_CONFIG = ".vendored/configs/git-semver.json"` constant
- Update `load_config()`: when no explicit `--config` flag, try vendored path first, fall back to `.semver/config.json`
- Filter `_vendor` key from loaded config (framework-owned metadata)
- `--config` CLI flag still works as explicit override (skips vendored lookup)

### No other changes needed
- `get_subdirectories()` already skips `_`-prefixed keys — no change needed
- All other config functions work on the filtered dict — transparent

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `VENDORED_CONFIG` constant added pointing to `.vendored/configs/git-semver.json`
- [ ] `load_config()` tries vendored path first when no explicit `--config`
- [ ] Falls back to `.semver/config.json` when vendored config doesn't exist
- [ ] `_vendor` key filtered out of loaded config
- [ ] `--config` flag overrides vendored lookup
- [ ] Subdirectory detection unaffected
- [ ] Tests added: vendored config path resolution, `_vendor` filtering, `--config` override, fallback behavior

## References

- See `docs/planning/epics/gsv-9ed5.md` "Config loading" section for target code
- Current load_config(): git-semver lines 38-45

title: Vendor V2 Contract Compliance
body: Make git-semver install.sh and runtime compliant with git-vendored v2 contract. Covers env vars, install dir, manifest emission, version file removal, self-registration removal, vendored config loading, tests, and docs. Planning: docs/planning/vendor-v2-compliance.md
